nodes.title: Return early when node is missing
nodes.why: This keeps traversal safe and gives a clean empty-string result for invalid input.
nodes.alternative: Throw an error when `node` is missing.
nodes.tradeoff: More tolerant behavior, but less strict about bad input.

nodes.title: Read text node values directly
nodes.why: Text nodes are the pieces we want, so we return their content immediately.
nodes.alternative: Traverse every node first, then filter text later.
nodes.tradeoff: Simple and direct, but relies on remembering `nodeType === 3` means text.

nodes.title: Use recursive DFS over children
nodes.why: Recursion naturally follows tree structure and appends child text in document order.
nodes.tradeoff: Very readable for trees, but deep trees can hit call-stack limits.

nodes.title: Use an explicit stack for traversal
nodes.why: This avoids recursion depth limits and keeps traversal fully iterative.
nodes.tradeoff: More robust for deep trees, with slightly more boilerplate.

nodes.title: Append text nodes and skip child logic
nodes.why: Once we read a text node, we can continue immediately because it has no useful child traversal for this task.
nodes.alternative: Handle text and non-text logic in one combined branch.
nodes.tradeoff: Clear control flow, but uses more explicit branching.

nodes.title: Push children right-to-left to preserve output order
nodes.why: Stack is LIFO, so reversing push order keeps traversal output left-to-right.
nodes.alternative: Push left-to-right and accept reversed processing.
nodes.tradeoff: Correct ordering with a tiny indexing loop.
